allow:
Bash(bd add "Extract pre-tax deductions from paystubs" --description "$\(cat <<''EOF''
## Goal
Automatically extract pre-tax deductions from paystubs and account for them in tax calculations.

## Deductions to Extract
- 401\(k\) contributions
- 403\(b\) contributions
- HSA contributions
- FSA contributions \(healthcare and dependent care\)
- Commuter benefits

## Requirements
1. Update PDF parser to extract these deduction amounts from paystubs
2. Subtract pre-tax deductions from gross income when calculating tax liability
3. Track YTD contributions per deduction type
4. Track against IRS contribution limits \(e.g., $23,500 for 401k, $4,300 for HSA\)
5. Warn when approaching limits

## Out of Scope
- Traditional IRA / Roth IRA \(future follow-on\)
- Recommendations for contribution optimization \(future follow-on\)

## Test Criteria
- [ ] Parser extracts 401k/403b contributions from sample fixture
- [ ] Parser extracts HSA/FSA contributions from sample fixture
- [ ] Tax calculation subtracts pre-tax deductions from gross
- [ ] YTD totals tracked per deduction type per person
- [ ] Warning displayed when within 10% of IRS limit
EOF
\)")

Bash(bd create "Hourly worker support for EOY projection" -d "$\(cat <<''EOF''
## Goal
Support accurate EOY projections for hourly workers whose pay varies by hours worked.

## Background
Salaried workers have consistent paychecks, but hourly workers'' withholding varies with hours. A short pay period shouldn''t drag down the projection.

## Requirements
1. Extract hours worked from paystubs \(for hourly workers\)
2. Calculate rolling average of hours to determine "typical" pay period
3. Normalize withholding to typical hours before using for projection
4. Anomaly detection — exclude outlier weeks from rolling average:
   - Unusually short weeks \(vacation, sick time\)
   - Unusually long weeks \(heavy overtime\)

## Example
If typical is 80 hours but last paycheck was 60 hours with $800 withheld:
- Normalized withholding = $800 * \(80/60\) = $1,067
- Use $1,067 for projection, not $800

## Test Criteria
- [ ] Hours worked extracted from paystub
- [ ] Rolling average calculated correctly
- [ ] Anomaly detection excludes outlier weeks
- [ ] Withholding normalized to typical hours for projection
EOF
\)" --deps "blocks:tax-d7g")

Bash(bd update tax-6sy -d "$\(cat <<''EOF''
## Goal
Automatically extract pre-tax deductions from paystubs and account for them in tax calculations.

## Deductions to Extract
- 401\(k\) contributions
- 403\(b\) contributions
- HSA contributions
- FSA contributions \(healthcare and dependent care\)
- Commuter benefits

## Requirements
1. Update PDF parser to extract these deduction amounts from paystubs
2. Subtract pre-tax deductions from gross income when calculating tax liability
3. Track YTD contributions per deduction type
4. Fetch IRS contribution limits and cache per year in SQLite \(same pattern as tax brackets\)
   - 401\(k\)/403\(b\): $23,500 for 2025
   - HSA: $4,300 individual / $8,550 family for 2025
   - FSA: $3,300 for 2025
5. Warn when approaching limits \(within 10%\)

## Out of Scope
- Traditional IRA / Roth IRA \(future follow-on\)
- Recommendations for contribution optimization \(future follow-on\)

## Test Criteria
- [ ] Parser extracts 401k/403b contributions from sample fixture
- [ ] Parser extracts HSA/FSA contributions from sample fixture
- [ ] Tax calculation subtracts pre-tax deductions from gross
- [ ] YTD totals tracked per deduction type per person
- [ ] Contribution limits fetched and cached per year
- [ ] Warning displayed when within 10% of IRS limit
EOF
\)")
